response:
Yes, it is possible to rotate the six light sources to illuminate the entire plane. Since each source illuminates a $60^{\circ}$ sector, the total angular coverage provided by all six sources is $6 \times 60^{\circ} = 360^{\circ}$. By positioning the sources at distinct points and rotating each beam to cover a specific, non-overlapping $60^{\circ}$ wedge of the $360^{\circ}$ circle surrounding the arrangement, the union of these sectors can cover every direction in the plane. Provided the points are not collinear in a way that creates an unavoidable shadow (which is avoidable with general placement and rotation), the entire plane can be illuminated.

P.S. This solution relies on the fact that the sum of the individual angles equals the full circle.
